conversation.content:
Search returned nothing useful. The physical principle is well established regardless: amyloid lag time τ scales as a power law of monomer concentration, τ ∝ m0^(-γ) (Knowles/Cohen master equation, secondary nucleation gives γ ≈ (n2+1)/2). So higher m0 → earlier onset. The breakthrough idea encodes this with center = c5/(1 + c9²·m0 + c6²·seed), which is monotonically decreasing in m0 and seed — physically correct and bounded ≥1 in the denominator.

Let me implement it carefully. I'll modify constants to 10, the gate, secondary term (drop the m0^c4 exponent), and initial_values.
